note: AFEW sanctuary for endangered Rothschild's giraffes; the raised feeding platform lets you hand-feed (and get kissed by!) giraffes and is generally accessible with tour assistance. A joyful, family-friendly highlight in Langata.

The Sheldrick Wildlife Trust's nursery for rescued orphan elephants; open one hour daily (11am-12pm), you watch the calves feed and mud-bathe from an open exhibition viewing area that is largely accessible. Book/register ahead. Deeply moving.

A genuine Big Five safari on the city's edge (lions, rhino, giraffe, zebra against the skyline); done as a game drive in an open-roof 4x4 - wheelchair/stroller-accessible safari vans can be arranged, so you experience it from the vehicle. Book an accessible operator.

The 'Out of Africa' author's colonial farmhouse at the foot of the Ngong Hills; small but wheelchair accessible via a wooden ramp at the back door, with knowledgeable guides. A quiet, characterful stop.

A ceramic-bead workshop employing single mothers and disadvantaged Kenyans ('kazuri' = small and beautiful); you can watch handcrafting and shop. Generally accessible and a meaningful, feel-good stop often paired with Karen sights.

National museum covering Kenya's nature, culture and prehistory (early-human fossils); a modern institution generally accessible with ramps/lifts - a solid rainy-day option. Confirm specifics on arrival.

Cultural centre showcasing traditional homesteads (bomas) and daily tribal dance performances; the theatre is reachable but the recreated village grounds have uneven/dirt surfaces. Come mainly for the show.

A raised boardwalk wildlife park at the Nairobi National Park gate (a preview of Kenya's animals); the elevated walkway is largely accessible - an easier, contained alternative to a full game drive. Good with kids.
An urban forest with trails, a waterfall and cafes; the main gravel/dirt paths are wide but unpaved, so manageable in parts with effort/assistance. A leafy break from the city.
Central park with a boating lake and city-skyline views; open flat areas are rollable for a photo stop, though upkeep varies. A quick central green stop, often part of a city tour.
